text.title: Plan Mode is read-only and produces a four-section plan
text.summary: While in Plan Mode the agent has only read tools; the plan it emits has steps, verification commands, an out-of-scope list, and a rollback note before any human approval can land.

section.title: Read-only planning surface
section.p: Plan Mode is enforced by the harness, not by the prompt. The agent's tool list is filtered: Edit, Write, MultiEdit, and any Bash command that mutates state are stripped. Read, Glob, Grep, and reference-fetching tools remain. The agent cannot accidentally start work because the verbs it would need are absent.

rule.statement: For any change scoped to more than three files OR an estimated runtime over ~5 minutes, the agent MUST enter Plan Mode first. The emitted plan MUST contain four sections in this order: numbered Steps, Verification commands (the exact shell invocations that prove success), Out-of-Scope (explicit exclusions), and Rollback (one-line revert command or "no rollback path" with reason). Approval is recorded as a human comment "approve" or harness-equivalent; the agent MUST NOT exit Plan Mode without it.
rule.rationale: Forcing four sections eliminates the two most common plan failures: missing verification (humans approve plans they cannot test) and missing rollback (humans approve plans they cannot reverse). Read-only enforcement removes the temptation to "start with one quick fix" before approval lands.

example.code:
# Plan: extract auth into its own service
## Steps 1. Create `services/auth/` package; move `apps/web/auth/*` into it. 2. Update import paths in `apps/web/`, `apps/api/`, `tests/`. 3. Add `services/auth/pyproject.toml`, register in workspace. 4. Add CI matrix entry for `services/auth/`.
## Verification - `pytest services/auth/ apps/web/ apps/api/ -x` - `ruff check services/ apps/` - `mypy services/auth/`
## Out of scope - Do not change session DB schema. - Do not touch frontend. - Do not refactor existing tests.
## Rollback - `git revert <merge-sha>` — package is additive, callers fall back to the old import path until then.
example.note: Four explicit sections; reviewer can grant or deny on evidence.

example.code: # Plan I'll move auth into its own service and update the imports. Should be quick.
example.note: No steps, no verification, no out-of-scope, no rollback — un-approvable.

antipattern.description: Agent runs `Bash("ls -R apps/")` "to gather context for planning" and the harness allows it because the command is read-only — but the agent then treats the read as a license to begin executing.
antipattern.reason: Plan Mode is a phase, not a tool subset; the constraint is "no plan-derived action until approval", not "Read tools imply consent".

section.reference: Plan skeleton with the four required sections.
Claude Code agentic workflow patterns (Plan Mode coverage).
